title: This company is actively hiring
description: Meet Benevity Benevity is the way the world does good, providing companies (and their employees) with technology to take social action on the issues they care about. Through giving, volunteering, grantmaking, employee resource groups and micro-actions, we help most of the Fortune 100 brands build better cultures and use their power for good. We’re also one of the first B Corporations in Canada, meaning we’re as committed to purpose as we are to profits. We have people working all over the world, including Canada, Spain, Switzerland, the United Kingdom, the United States and more! High-Level Overview Are you a visionary creative ready to help shape the visual identity of a global brand? As our Creative Director, you will be the guardian of the Benevity brand, responsible for defining and evolving our brand’s visual and digital language to inspire action and drive social impact. You are equally at home concepting a campaign as you are building a project plan — a rare blend of creative and operational leader who thrives on both the big idea and the details that bring it to life. You will work at the intersection of strategy and execution, leading high-stakes creative projects and mentoring designers to elevate our profile as a category leader and the world's enterprise impact platform. This role is ideal for a leader who "grew up digital” and knows how to run a tight creative operation. Managing the processes, contributing to the work and mentoring designers to push their craft, leading high-stakes creative projects, and evolving our brand’s visual language to inspire action and drive global social impact. If you’re ready to take your creative career to the next level, we would love to hear from you! What You'll Do Creative Strategy: Develop and implement a comprehensive brand design strategy aligned with Benevity's business objectives to improve brand equity and visual consistency across all touchpoints. Visual Identity Leadership: Lead the evolution of Benevity’s visual identity system, across all media, ensuring it remains modern, accessible, and deeply resonant with our global audience. Creative Execution: Lead day to day execution and output of creative team and brainstorming for major marketing programs, product launches, and events. Develop a creative base for “big Ideas” that convert product and social impact data into emotional, thumb-stopping creative concepts. Direct, design and produce high-quality creative assets for major campaigns, product launches, and brand initiatives, ensuring excellence in typography, color, engagement (digital) and layout. Operational Leadership & Workload Management: Own the creative team's intake process, project prioritization, and capacity planning to ensure the right work gets done at the right time and that our teams can work at the speed of impact. Build and maintain systems that give the team clarity on priorities, deadlines, and bandwidth — balancing urgent requests against longer-horizon brand-building work without sacrificing quality. Collaboration across the business: Partner with Product, Marketing, Sales and People teams to ensure appropriate input and creative briefs that lend themselves to a unified brand experience across the entire customer and employee journey. Work with and influence stakeholders at all levels, including executives. Web oversight: Partner with the Digital Marketing team to deliver a high-performing, on-brand web experience, establishing and enforcing shared standards that drive improvements in performance, accessibility, and creative consistency. Mentorship & Influence: Provide creative direction and mentorship to the design team, supporting their design time while fostering a culture of innovation, excellence, experimentation and continuous learning. Team and Agency Management: In addition to managing internal team resources, oversee budget and external creative agencies and vendors to ensure timely project execution that meets Benevity’s high standards f
score_reason: Senior CD role with global scope and Canada presence, but missing critical details on remote status, compensation, and full job description weakens the fit.

title: Apply with resume & profile
description: This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Creative Lead, Lifecycle & Organic Growth in Canada. This is an exciting opportunity for a creative and strategic marketer who thrives at the intersection of storytelling, customer engagement, and organic growth. In this role, you will shape the voice and emotional connection of a fast-growing consumer platform used by millions of customers. You’ll lead the evolution of lifecycle communications across email, SMS, in-app messaging, and emerging organic channels, transforming transactional interactions into meaningful brand experiences. Working closely with product, growth, design, and customer insights teams, you’ll craft messaging that builds trust, drives retention, and strengthens long-term customer loyalty. This position offers a highly autonomous and collaborative environment where creativity, experimentation, and first-principles thinking are valued. It’s an ideal opportunity for someone who enjoys building from the ground up and making a visible impact on brand perception and customer growth. Accountabilities Lead the development and evolution of brand voice, tone, and messaging across lifecycle and organic growth channels. Create and optimize customer communications including email campaigns, SMS messaging, push notifications, in-app copy, onboarding flows, and retention programs. Collaborate with cross-functional teams including Product, Design, Growth, and Customer Insights to align messaging with user needs and business goals. Build customer trust and engagement through empathetic, human-centered storytelling and value-driven content. Analyze customer feedback, behavioral insights, and qualitative research to improve messaging effectiveness and customer experience. Develop and execute creative organic growth initiatives across channels such as social media, online communities, and content-driven engagement platforms. Contribute to customer retention, brand affinity, and long-term conversion through thoughtful and emotionally resonant communication strategies. Continuously experiment with messaging approaches, audience segmentation, and creative formats to optimize lifecycle performance. Requirements 5+ years of experience in creative marketing, lifecycle marketing, copywriting, or organic growth roles within consumer-focused environments. Strong expertise in writing compelling copy for email, SMS, in-app experiences, social media, and customer engagement campaigns. Proven ability to translate complex or technical topics into clear, engaging, and relatable messaging. Experience building or evolving brand voice and customer communication strategies in fast-paced or high-growth organizations. Deep understanding of audience engagement, community dynamics, and organic growth channels. Strong collaboration and communication skills with the ability to work effectively across product, marketing, and design teams. Analytical mindset with the ability to leverage qualitative insights and customer behavior data to inform creative decisions. Comfortable operating independently in a hands-on individual contributor role with significant ownership and autonomy. Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience preferred. Portfolio demonstrating creative writing, lifecycle campaigns, and brand storytelling experience required. Benefits Competitive base salary ranging from CA$125,000 to CA$175,000 annually depending on experience, skills, and location. Equity opportunities as part of the total compensation package. Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance coverage. RRSP plan with employer matching contributions. Paid time off and paid parental leave programs. Wellness benefits and flexible remote work environment. Access to professional growth opportunities within a high-growth technology company. Collaborative and innovation-driven culture with significant ownership and impact. How Jobgether Works We use an AI-powered matc
